zh.lifeSummary: 當Philippa Trenowith於大約1450年出生在英國可瓦爾聖 邁克爾 吉維爾時，她的父親John Trenoweth25歲，母親Honor Tregarthin24歲。她於大約1465年與William Kayle結婚。她去世於1520年7月31日在她的家鄉享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: Trenowith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Cornish: locative name from one or more of at least twenty places in Cornwall named with Cornish tre ‘house, settlement’ + nowyth ‘new’, with the spellings Trenewth, Trenouth, Trenowah, Trenower, Trenoweth, Trenowth, Trenute, or Trenuth.

nameMeanings.name: Philippa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of Philip . In England during the Middle Ages the vernacular name Philip was borne by women as well as men, but female bearers were distinguished in Latin records by this form. It was not, however, used as a regular given name until the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Philippa Trenowith was born about 1450, in St Michael Penkivel, Cornwall, England, her father, John Trenoweth, was 25 and her mother, Honor Tregarthin, was 24. She married William Kayle about 1465. She died on 31 July 1520, in her hometown, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philippa Trenowith est née environ 1450, à St Michael Penkivel, Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, John Trenoweth, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Honor Tregarthin, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé William Kayle environ 1465. Elle est décédée le 31 juillet 1520, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans.
